rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK SPENCER III VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Answer Of Defendant Park Mechanical, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 24, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiff's motion to strike answer of defendant Park Mechanical, Inc.: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. =(503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MARY BETH PERNIZ VS. DURO DYNE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Western Plumbing And Heating Co., Inc. S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 24, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Western Plumbing & Heating Co., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party's request. =(503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BEATRICE KEAYS ET AL VS. 50 BEALE INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 24, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendants Paramount Group, Inc.'s and 50 Beale Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendants' special demurrer to the First (negligence) cause of action is overruled. The SAC states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for negligence and is not uncertain. This Court already made this ruling on defendants' demurrer to plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint, after which plaintiffs did not have leave to amend the negligence cause of action. Defendants' special demurrer to the Second (negligence per se) cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. The SAC fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for negligence per se. Further, plaintiffs have already had an opportunity to amend this cause of action, did not attempt to demonstrate how this cause of action is properly alleged, did not request leave to amend and did not demonstrate a reasonable possibility that they can state a proper cause of action. Defendants' special demurrer to the Third (strict liability) is overruled as moot. Plaintiffs state that they have agreed to dismiss this cause of action against defendants. However, the Court does not see a dismissal on file. If a dismissal is not filed within 10 days, defendants may submit correspondence to the Court, copying plaintiffs' counsel, notifying the Court of this and submitting an updated proposed order that sustains the special demurrer to this cause of action without leave to amend. Defendants have 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)
